title: 1.1: Tests that FromData delegates directly to the detector when the result cache is nil
1.2: Tests that FromData does not write/update the result cache when verify and forceCacheUpdate are false
1.3: Tests that FromData populates the cache when verify is false but forceCacheUpdate is true
1.4: Tests that FromData returns cached verification results when all results are cache hits
1.5: Tests that FromData performs remote verification when a cache miss occurs
1.6: Tests that FromData bypasses result-cache lookups when forceCacheUpdate is true
1.7: Tests that cache keys include detector type so same Raw credential with different detector types creates separate cache entries

1.8: Tests that the cache key includes detector type such that the same RawV2 credential found by two different detector types produces two distinct cache entries. (e.g. the same RawV2 + different detector type, not different RawV2.)
1.9: Tests that FromData returns results with verification disabled (e.g., Verified is false, no cached verification is applied) when verify and forceCacheUpdate are false

1.10: Tests that when `verify=true` and `forceCacheUpdate=true`, a pre-existing cache entry for the same result key(s) is replaced/overwritten with the newly verified value(s) (i.e., the test pre-populates cache using the same computed key(s), asserting the stored value(s) changed to match fresh verification output).

1.11: Tests that when `verify=true`, `forceCacheUpdate=false`, and at least one result is missing from cache, FromData writes verified results into cache for the missing key(s), asserting the cache contents include entries for the previously-missing result(s) after the call.
1.12: Tests that when verification results are served from cache, verification error information (if present) is asserted to propagate from cache into returned results
1.13: Tests that the "same credential, different detector type" cache-key scenarios assert the returned result preserves the second detector’s DetectorType (preventing cross-type cache contamination)
2.1: Places the new tests for in pkg/verificationcache/verification_cache_test.go
3.1: All tests use the Go testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the TestTypeName_MethodName_Scenario naming pattern
3.3: All tests use testify assert or require packages for assertions
4.1: Uses a detector stub/mock that implements the detectors.Detector interface for testing FromData

4.2: Tests that in the verification-enabled cache-miss scenario, the test explicitly asserts on returned results’ verification outputs (e.g., Verified and/or verification error fields), not only on list length, call count, or cache-hit/miss counters.
